name: Eating Atmosphere
paraphrase: Build the eating place around a solid common table large enough to hold the whole group, with a low pool of light directly over it and a clear enclosure of walls or half-walls that keeps the diners gathered. Alexander argues that the act of sharing a meal needs a definite spatial focus, because a bright, undefined or oversized room scatters the conversation and turns a meal into a transaction rather than a small daily ritual.
